title_id: Hadis Kedua: Iman kepada Hari Akhir — Nama, Peristiwa, dan Tiupan Sangkakala

' ফব' " .,a • '-""-:'.' ॲ.r- (/· Q.) ୪ "But no, by your Lord, they can have no faith until they make you judge in all disputes between them, and find in themselves no resistance against your decisions, and accept them with full submission" (al-Nisaa 65). The believer should realize that the sending of messengers for the benefit and guidance of mankind is a great blessing from Allah. The knowledge that they conveyed is knowledge that is beyond the grasp of the human intellect by itself since it deals with matters of the unseen. Any cursory study of history would demonstrate that mankind is not able to find the absolute and complete truth with respect to God and the worship of Him except through a reliable source that has the necessary knowledge from the proper source, and that is the messenger receiving his knowledge from Allah alone.3 1 Abdullah al-Muslih and Salaah al-Saawi, Ma La Yasa'u al-Muslim Jahla (Islamic Foundation of America, 1995), p. 59. 2 Abdur-Rahmaan Abdul-Khaliq, The General Prescripts of Belief in the Quran and Sunnah (The Majliss of al-Haqq Publication Society, 1986), p. 1 8. 3 Cf., Qutb, pp. 226-7. Hadith #2: The Hadith of Jibreel Indeed, mankind is in more need of their guidance than they are in need of food and drink. If they are void of food and drink for any period of time, they will die, implying loss of this life. But if they are refusing Allah's guidance through the messengers, they will lose the everlasting bounties of the Hereafter. Unfortunately for humans, in every era and in every generation, many of them think that they possess a great mind and intellect, therefore, they are not in need of any other human to guide them- even if that human were a messenger of Allah. They think they can find their own way. This arrogance and attributing to their intellects what is well beyond its reach will lead to their final destruction-- unless they repent, accept the messengers, believe in what they brought and follow their guidance. "the Last Day" "The Last Day" is called such because there will be no new day after it, as the people of Paradise shall be in their abode as will the people of Hell. Among its other names are "The Day of Resurrection," "The Reality," "The Event," "The Day of Judgment," and "The Overwhelming." This is the greatest day that mankind shall pass through. Indeed, it will be the gravest and most fearful day. A person's new life will be decided on that day. It will mark a new beginning for each and every soul. This new step may lead to eternal bliss or eternal damnation. 1 Belief in the Last Day implies belief in everything that the Quran or the Prophet (peace be upon him) has stated about the events of that Day and thereafter. There are some general aspects (resurrection, judgment and reward, Paradise and Hell) that every Muslim should be aware of and believe in with certainty. There are also more detailed aspects that the Quran or the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) mentioned. The more one has knowledge of that Day and its surrounding events, the greater the effect this belief will have on him. Hence, it is highly recommended for each individual Muslim to learn about the events that occur prior to and on the Day of Resurrection. As recorded in Sahih Muslim, before the Day of Judgment and the destruction of this earth, Allah will send a wind softer than silk, coming from Yemen, that will take the souls of every individual who has even the slightest amount of faith in his heart. Therefore, the events of the end of the earth will only be lived through by the worst of people, those with no faith whatsoever. One of the first events to occur is the sun rising from the West. At that time, all those people will declare their faith but it will be of no avail to them. Then the Hom shall be blown and all on this earth shall die. Allah says, 1 For Quranic/rational arguments for the existence of the Hereafter, see Idris, pp. 1 1-16. Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi i-ԮT ;11 ;. ԯ԰jԱԲT J, ;.j ԳpT J, ;. J./.a/• )\\ J, ԍj / ,,,. ,,,. ,,,. "And the trumpet will be blown, and all who are in the heavens and all who are on the earth will swoon away, except him whom Allah wills" (al-Zumar 68). This earth and heaven shall then be destroyed.
content_id:
Rukun Iman Kelima — Hari Akhir: 'Hari Akhir' disebut demikian karena tidak akan ada hari baru setelahnya, karena penghuni surga akan berada di tempat tinggal mereka sebagaimana penghuni neraka. Di antara nama-namanya yang lain adalah 'Hari Kebangkitan,' 'Al-Haqqah,' 'Al-Waqi'ah,' 'Hari Kiamat,' dan 'Al-Ghasyiyah.' Ini adalah hari terbesar yang akan dilalui umat manusia — sungguh akan menjadi hari yang paling berat dan paling menakutkan. Kehidupan baru seseorang akan ditentukan pada hari itu. Ini akan menandai awal baru bagi setiap jiwa. Langkah baru ini dapat mengarah pada kebahagiaan abadi atau kesengsaraan abadi.

Iman kepada Hari Akhir menyiratkan iman kepada segala sesuatu yang Al-Quran atau Nabi sebutkan tentang peristiwa-peristiwa pada Hari itu dan sesudahnya. Ada beberapa aspek umum — kebangkitan, penghakiman dan ganjaran, surga, dan neraka — yang harus diketahui dan diyakini setiap Muslim dengan pasti. Ada juga aspek-aspek yang lebih rinci yang disebutkan Al-Quran atau Rasulullah. Semakin seseorang memiliki pengetahuan tentang Hari itu dan peristiwa-peristiwa di sekitarnya, semakin besar dampak keyakinan ini terhadapnya.

Sebagaimana tercatat dalam Shahih Muslim, sebelum Hari Kiamat dan hancurnya bumi ini, Allah akan mengirim angin yang lebih lembut dari sutra, datang dari Yaman, yang akan mengambil ruh setiap individu yang memiliki bahkan sedikit pun iman dalam hatinya. Oleh karena itu, peristiwa-peristiwa akhir bumi hanya akan dialami oleh orang-orang yang paling buruk, mereka yang sama sekali tidak memiliki iman. Salah satu peristiwa pertama yang terjadi adalah matahari terbit dari Barat. Pada saat itu semua orang-orang tersebut akan menyatakan iman mereka tetapi itu tidak akan bermanfaat bagi mereka. Kemudian sangkakala ditiup dan semua yang ada di bumi akan mati. Allah berfirman: 'Dan ditiuplah sangkakala, maka matilah semua yang ada di langit dan semua yang ada di bumi kecuali yang Allah kehendaki' (az-Zumar: 68). Bumi dan langit ini kemudian akan hancur.

Umat manusia lebih membutuhkan petunjuk para rasul daripada mereka membutuhkan makanan dan minuman. Jika mereka kekurangan makanan dan minuman untuk jangka waktu tertentu, mereka akan mati — yang berarti kehilangan kehidupan dunia ini. Namun jika mereka menolak petunjuk Allah melalui para rasul, mereka akan kehilangan kenikmatan-kenikmatan akhirat yang kekal. Sayangnya bagi manusia, di setiap zaman dan di setiap generasi, banyak dari mereka yang berpikir bahwa mereka memiliki akal dan intelek yang besar sehingga mereka tidak membutuhkan manusia lain untuk membimbing mereka — bahkan jika manusia itu adalah rasul Allah. Mereka berpikir dapat menemukan jalan mereka sendiri. Kesombongan ini dan menisbatkan kepada intelek mereka apa yang jauh melampaui jangkauannya akan mengarah pada kehancuran akhir mereka — kecuali jika mereka bertobat, menerima para rasul, beriman kepada apa yang mereka bawa, dan mengikuti petunjuk mereka.
